name: golang-github-azuread-microsoft-authentication-library-for-go-dev
title: golang-github-azuread-microsoft-authentication-library-for-go-dev
summary: Sign in users or apps with Microsoft Accounts or Azure AD identities
description:
Sign in users or apps with Microsoft Accounts or Azure AD identities
The Microsoft Authentication Library (MSAL) for Go is part of the Microsoft
identity platform for developers v2.0. It allows you to sign in users or apps
with Microsoft identities (Azure AD and Microsoft Accounts) and obtain tokens
to call Microsoft APIs such as Microsoft Graph or your own APIs registered with
the Microsoft identity platform. It is built using industry standard OAuth2 and
OpenID Connect protocols.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use golang-github-azuread-microsoft-authentication-library-for-go-dev because Sign in users or apps with Microsoft Accounts or Azure AD identities.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use golang-github-azuread-microsoft-authentication-library-for-go-dev as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair golang-github-azuread-microsoft-authentication-library-for-go-dev with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `golang-github-azuread-microsoft-authentication-library-for-go-dev --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
